The [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn]] '''LD-1 target blaster rifle''' (also called a '''targeting blaster rifle''')<ref name="The Unknown Regions" /> was technically a [[sporting blaster rifle]], but in actuality it was nearly as powerful as a military-grade [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] like the [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11]]. This was intentional - the goal of the project was to create a weapon that approaches the damage of a blaster rifle without the pesky legal restrictions that come with such weapons.

The LD-1, with its long barrel, was quite useful as a [[Sniper rifle/Legends|sniper rifle]], and could be disassembled into a carrying case like most such weapons. Additionally, the weapon featured an integrated [[Macroscope/Legends|macroscope]] and [[autosteady gyro-gimbal]]s that drastically helped with balance and sighting.<ref name="RoE">''[[Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook]]''</ref>

There were rumors of an offshoot of the weapon, the LD-1a, that was produced in limited numbers for the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]], which took a few cues from the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] [[X-45 sniper rifle]], namely its overfocused beam.
